activities_projects.title: Theme Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central theme and moral lesson of the poem
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify the main lesson about selfishness and generosity
Write 3–4 sentences explaining the theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the poem’s moral and central idea

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze key characters and their traits
steps: Write the names of the main characters (Saint Peter and the woman)
Describe their qualities, actions, and motivations
Explain how their behavior contributes to the story’s moral

activities_projects.title: Event Sequencing
activities_projects.objective: Understand the chronological order of events
steps: List the key events in the poem
Arrange them in the order they occur
Explain why the sequence is important to the story

activities_projects.title: Imagery and Description
activities_projects.objective: Identify descriptive elements in the poem
steps: Find lines describing the Northland, reindeer, and snowy scenes
Explain how these images help visualize the story
Write a short paragraph summarizing the imagery
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances visualization and understanding of imagery

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn new words and improve comprehension
steps: Find words like harness, scanty, provoke, boring
Write their meanings
Use them in sentences related to the poem

activities_projects.title: Moral Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Connect the poem’s lesson to real life
steps: Think of situations where generosity and sharing are important
Write 3–4 sentences connecting them to the poem
Discuss your ideas with classmates

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand consequences of actions in the poem
steps: Identify the woman’s selfish actions
Write the consequences she faces (turning into a woodpecker)
Explain how her actions led to these consequences

activities_projects.title: Creative Story Writing
steps: Imagine you are the woman after being turned into a woodpecker
Write a short paragraph about your life in the forest
Include what you eat and how you live

activities_projects.title: Group Discussion
steps: Discuss why Saint Peter punished the woman
Share your ideas on fairness and justice
Write a summary of the discussion
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Illustration Activity
steps: Draw the woman baking cakes or as a woodpecker
Label the important elements
Share your illustration in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creativity and understanding of the story
